messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] visualizing microarray data together with metabolites in pathways?
messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel] visualizing microarray data together with metabolites in pathways?
messages.body_plain:
Hello,

Recently I noticed that there were some packages coming out for visualizing
microarray data in some pathway background (e.g. KEGG). I am thinking if it
is possible to also include the quantitative information of metabolites in
the colored pathway graphs. (As the metabolites are also very important
nodes in KEGG pathways) 

Has anybody touched this? Any ideas on this?

Thx a lot.

Kind regards,
Hao
___________________________________
Hao HU
phd student
Nutrition and Physiology Institute
Technical University of Munich
Am Forum 5 D-85350 Freising Germany
phone +498161713555
fax      +498161713999
hao.hu at wzw.tum.de
www.nutrition.tum.de

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] websites shutdown at 2pm
messages.body_plain:
Hi,

For maintenance purpose, we will need to reboot our servers
hosting bioconductor.org and cran.fhcrc.org today at 2pm (Seattle
time). All services hosted on these servers will be down for 10-20
minutes. Note that during this time, biocLite() will not work.

Thanks for your comprehension and sorry for the inconvenience.

Cheers,
H.

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] classes for gene sets
messages.body_plain:
Hi all,

I'm writing some functions dealing with sets or lists of genes. There 
has been a discussion about classes for representing gene sets:
http://wiki.fhcrc.org/bioc/GeneSet_Class_Discussion
Has someone implemented the discussed ideas or do I have to implement my 
own classes? It seems that the discussion has been stop a few month ago.

Regards,
Hans-Ulrich

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] Maanova License
messages.body_plain:
Dear all,

I am asking here the question as I have already mailed twice
mailto:maanova at jax.org (from the website [1]) without any answer...

Looking into the code of the version 1.7.1 and 1.9.0 it appears that
every where it is written :

# Licensed under the GNU General Public License version 2 (June, 1991)

And the DESCRIPTION file says:
License: GPL version 2 or later

As we are trying to get some of the Bioconductor library into Fedora
[2], I would like to ask if anyone of you know which one of the previous
licenses is the correct one for this library.

Thanks in advance for your help,

Regards,
P.Yves

[1]http://www.jax.org/staff/churchill/labsite/software/Rmaanova/
[2]https://bugzilla.redhat.com/show_bug.cgi?id=244234

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] netCDF on pelham
messages.body_plain:
Hi,

for XCMS we'd need netCDF on MacOSX pelham,
i.e. shlibs and netcdf.h. There is a package on fink.sf.net.

Is there a way to put that on pelham, 
or do we have to include a netcsf.dylib in the XCMS package ?

Yours,
Steffen


-- 
IPB Halle                    AG Massenspektrometrie & Bioinformatik
Dr. Steffen Neumann          http://www.IPB-Halle.DE
Weinberg 3                   http://msbi.bic-gh.de
06120 Halle                  New Phone number !
                             Tel. +49 (0) 345 5582 - 1470
sneumann(at)IPB-Halle.DE     Fax. +49 (0) 345 5582 - 1409
